expectations.text: Defines prior with correct support for all 4 parameters (beta>0, gamma>0, 0<i0<1, 0<p<1)
expectations.evidence: Lines 48-51: beta = rng.gamma(shape=2.0, scale=0.3) (positive support), gamma = rng.gamma(shape=2.0, scale=0.15) (positive support), i0 = rng.beta(a=2.0, b=50.0) (0,1 support), p = rng.beta(a=5.0, b=2.0) (0,1 support).

expectations.text: Uses bf.Adapter() with explicit constraint handling
expectations.evidence: Lines 142-156: adapter = bf.Adapter() with .constrain() calls for all four parameters.

expectations.text: Calls .constrain() with lower=0 for beta and gamma
Calls .constrain() with lower=0, upper=1 for i0 and p
expectations.evidence: Line 149: .constrain("i0", lower=0, upper=1) and Line 150: .constrain("p", lower=0, upper=1).

expectations.text: Routes the daily case counts through summary_variables (not inference_conditions)
expectations.evidence: Line 156: .concatenate(["daily_cases"], into="summary_variables"). No inference_conditions key used anywhere in the adapter.

expectations.text: Uses a summary network appropriate for sequential data (TimeSeriesTransformer, TimeSeriesNetwork, or SetTransformer if treating days as exchangeable is justified and documented)
expectations.evidence: Line 120: summary_net = bf.networks.TimeSeriesTransformer(...). Analysis notes (line 19) justify the choice: 'Daily case counts have temporal ordering -- day 5 vs day 25 matters.'

expectations.text: Starts with the Small model configuration from references/model-sizes.md
expectations.evidence: No reference to 'Small' configuration or references/model-sizes.md anywhere in the code or notes. The architecture uses custom values (embed_dims=(32,32), num_heads=(2,2), mlp_widths=(64,64), FlowMatching widths=(128,128)) that are not sourced from the skill's model-sizes reference.

expectations.text: Sets summary_dim to approximately 2x the number of parameters (around 8)
expectations.evidence: Line 121: summary_dim=8. Line 116 comment: '4 parameters -> summary_dim = 2 * num_params = 8'.

expectations.text: Passes validation_data= to the fit_* call
Runs in-silico diagnostics before any real-data inference
expectations.evidence: Section 7 (lines 236-250) runs in-silico diagnostics with 300 simulated test datasets. Section 8 (lines 258+) performs inference on synthetic test data. Diagnostics come first.

expectations.text: Checks all 4 parameters individually against house thresholds
expectations.evidence: Lines 280-287 check individual parameter posterior coverage (95% CI containing truth), and lines 384-395 check constraint violations per parameter. However, neither section compares against predefined 'house thresholds' (e.g., R-squared > X, RMSE < Y, calibration error < Z). The code reports metrics but does not programmatically check them against threshold values.

expectations.text: Does NOT flatten the time series into inference_conditions
expectations.evidence: Line 156: daily_cases is routed into summary_variables. The string 'inference_conditions' does not appear anywhere in the code. The time series is preserved via .as_time_series(["daily_cases"]) on line 145.
